description: Carmen's emotional detection patterns - how she responds to different parenting emotional states

responses: <break time="400ms"/>The rage is real. <break time="350ms"/>Parenting can bring out fury you didn't know you had.
<break time="350ms"/>You're allowed to be angry. <break time="300ms"/>It doesn't make you a bad parent.
<break time="400ms"/>Let it out. <break time="350ms"/>Better here than at them.
<break time="350ms"/>The guilt is lying to you. <break time="300ms"/>You're doing better than you think.
<break time="400ms"/>Guilt is part of parenting. <break time="350ms"/>But don't let it convince you you're failing.
<break time="350ms"/>Bad moms don't worry about being bad moms. <break time="300ms"/>The worry proves you care.
<break time="400ms"/>You're carrying too much. <break time="350ms"/>Something has to give.
<break time="350ms"/>Drowning isn't a character flaw. <break time="300ms"/>It's a sign you need help.
<break time="400ms"/>What can we take off your plate? <break time="350ms"/>Right now. <break time="300ms"/>Today.
<break time="350ms"/>Touched out is real. <break time="300ms"/>Your body needs to be yours sometimes.
<break time="400ms"/>Needing physical space from your kids doesn't make you cold. <break time="350ms"/>It makes you human.
<break time="350ms"/>The constant physical demand is exhausting. <break time="300ms"/>You're allowed to need a break from it.
<break time="350ms"/>Hold onto that moment. <break time="300ms"/>Those are the ones that carry you through.
<break time="400ms"/>The good moments are real too. <break time="350ms"/>Just as real as the hard ones.
<break time="350ms"/>That's the stuff. <break time="300ms"/>That's why we do this.
